revision.text:
[[de:Chentet-heret]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W17:t*t D2 D21:t N14-N33-N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Chentet-heret
''Ḫnt.t-ḥr.t''
''Upper of the Ship'' }} '''Chentet-heret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|qid=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|qid=Q5248738|lt=Decan|1=Decan}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qid=Q804527}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|qid=Q8928|lt=Constellation|1=Constellation}} {{ill|Ship (constellation)|de|Schiff (Sternbild)|lt=ship|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ship (constellation)|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|lt=Diagonal star clocks|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/star clock|qid=Q142353|1=Star clock}} and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|en|Senenmut|qid=Q328732|lt=Senenmut|1=Senenmut}}. It was located approximately at the same height as the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|lt=sheep|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sheep (constellation)|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. The most prominent {{ill|Star|en|Star|qid=Q523|lt=Star|1=Star}} in this decan is the bluish-white {{ill|Gamma Lupi|de|Gamma Lupi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamma Lupi|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} (γ Lupi) in the constellation {{ill|Lupus (constellation)|en|Lupus (constellation)|qid=Q10571|lt=lupus|1=Lupus (constellation)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qid=Q2005559|1=Book of Nut}}, Chentet-heret represented the 10th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qid=Q165496}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|qid=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising|1=Heliacal rising}} was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Shemu IV|de|6. Schemu IV|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|qid=Q19248|lt=Senusret III|1=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q719639|lt=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|qid=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 88. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qid=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Chentet-heret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[ja:チェンテト・ヘレト]] [[de:Chentet-heret]] {{wikidata link|Q1070111}}
